purpose: G5 structured missing-channel robustness check for Level 2 recognition.

warnings: nearfield uses Ex/Ey/Ez; Python can convert to theta/phi if coordinates are correct

structured_dropout_cases.description: Per-sample correlated sensor-node dropout: selected sensors lose all frequencies and both polarizations.
Severe per-sample sensor-node dropout at 25 percent of selected layout sensors.
Per-sample sensor-frequency dropout where theta and phi channels fail together.
Per-sample contiguous azimuth-sector dropout over a 60 degree sector.

training_cases.description: Complex additive channel noise at 20 dB SNR.
Complex additive channel noise at 10 dB SNR.
Independent complex phase jitter with 15 deg standard deviation.
Independent complex phase jitter with 45 deg standard deviation.
Random theta/phi channel dropout with zero-fill at 10 percent.
Random theta/phi channel dropout with zero-fill at 25 percent.
Combined 10 dB noise, 15 deg phase jitter, and 10 percent channel dropout.

boundary: Level 2 CST-derived structured dropout evidence only. It does not replace real measurement calibration, full-wave airframe validation, or true-monitor reconstruction gates.
